dependencies: Two suppliers accounted for 65.4% and 6.3% of total purchases for fiscal year 2024
Ablecom manufactured approximately 93.6% of chassis included in products sold during fiscal year 2024
Certain materials used in manufacturing are available from a limited number of suppliers
Reliance on Ablecom and Compuware for contract manufacturing services and inventory warehousing
risks: Concentration risk with limited number of suppliers for certain components
Supply chain disruptions due to global economic conditions, regional conflicts, pandemics
Shortages of critical materials (GPUs, CPUs, memory, SSDs) during periods of high demand
Dependence on Taiwan-based manufacturing facilities subject to geopolitical risks
Potential inability to qualify substitute suppliers quickly if relationships deteriorate

regulatory_risks: U.S. export control restrictions targeting China's semiconductor and supercomputing industries (October 2022, November 2023, January 2025)
Export licensing requirements for advanced GPUs and integrated circuits (NVIDIA A100, H100)
Worldwide authorization requirements for advanced computing products (January 2025)
Per-country and per-customer limits on advanced computing product exports
Trade sanctions enforced by U.S. Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
Export controls enforced by U.S. Commerce Department's Bureau of Industry and Security
GDPR compliance requirements in European Union
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
Data localization laws in Russia and China (Personal Information Protection Law)
Environmental regulations (EU eco-design requirements for servers, California Proposition 65)
Section 1502 of Dodd Frank Act concerning conflict minerals
OECD Pillar Two global minimum corporate tax (15%)
Potential new tariffs and trade restrictions under changing U.S. administration
Compliance with anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act)
Product safety and security regulations across multiple jurisdictions

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions on Russia, Belarus, and restricted areas in Ukraine (no sales since February 2022)
Export control restrictions on China including Hong Kong for advanced semiconductors
Restrictions on entities in China placed on U.S. government export control lists
Potential escalation of U.S.-China trade tensions and further export restrictions
Risk of being unable to provide warranty or service obligations due to licensing requirements
Deemed export control limitations impacting overseas operations
Potential foreign government responses (e.g., China) impacting supply chain or market access
Restrictions on high-end GPU products for AI applications in multiple countries including Middle East
Risk of customers or channel partners not adhering to trade compliance requirements
